description:
You are on-call as a database reliability engineer and you need to perform a very small
    API health-check of the internal “backup-service” that delivers backup status and
    artifacts through a simple HTTP interface which runs *locally* on port 9090.

    1. First, start the local service that is already present on the machine in the file  
       /home/user/mock_backup_api.py.  
       • It must listen on 0.0.0.0, port 9090.  
       • Keep it running for the whole check (backgrounding it is fine).  

    2. Send exactly **five** HTTP calls with curl – nothing more, nothing less – in the
       order shown below.  
       a.  GET  /status  
       b.  GET  /last-backup  
       c.  GET  /list  
       d.  POST /trigger-backup            (an empty JSON body is acceptable)  
       e.  GET  /download/2  

    3. After each curl call, append one single line to the log file
       /home/user/backup_test/backup_api_test.log  
       (create the directory backup_test first if it does not exist).

       • Every log line **must** follow this character-exact pattern, including the pipes:  
         YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ|<METHOD> <PATH>|<HTTP_STATUS>|<BYTES>  

         Example (do **NOT** hard-code these values, they are only illustrative):  
         2023-08-29T12:01:15Z|GET /status|200|34

       • The timestamp has to be in UTC (use the literal “Z” suffix).  
       • <BYTES> is the byte length of the response body *exactly as received*.  
       • Write one log line per request; therefore the final file will contain **5** lines.

    4. When the five requests are finished, create the file  
       /home/user/backup_test/summary.txt with **exactly five lines** in this order:

          /status: PASS  
          /last-backup: PASS  
          /list: PASS  
          /trigger-backup: PASS  
          /download/2: PASS  

       Use precisely that spelling, punctuation and capitalisation; one summary
       line per endpoint, nothing else.

    5. Do not print anything to stdout except the normal curl output that scrolls by
       when you invoke the commands.  All verification work must rely solely on the two
       files mentioned above.

    What the automated grader will look for:
       • /home/user/mock_backup_api.py is (or was) running listening on port 9090.  
       • /home/user/backup_test/backup_api_test.log exists, has 5 correctly-formatted
         lines whose HTTP status codes are all 200 and whose byte-counts match the
         real responses served by the API.  
       • /home/user/backup_test/summary.txt exists and has the exact five lines shown,
         in the exact order shown.  

    Notes & constraints:
       • You do NOT need root privileges.  
       • No external network calls are necessary; everything stays on localhost.  
       • Feel free to use &, nohup, disown or another mechanism to keep the server
         alive while you run the curl commands.  
       • Use standard GNU core utilities only (curl, date, mkdir, echo, etc.).  
       • A total of 5–10 terminal commands is expected; fewer or more is fine as long
         as the required artefacts are produced exactly as described.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth values for the automated checker.
        These must never be revealed to the agent.
    -->
    <server-script-path>/home/user/mock_backup_api.py</server-script-path>
    <server-port>9090</server-port>

    <!--  Exact JSON bodies served by the mock API  -->
    <endpoint path="/status"           length="34"  body='{"service":"backup","status":"OK"}'/>
    <endpoint path="/last-backup"      length="38"  body='{"last_backup":"2023-08-28T02:14:26Z"}'/>
    <endpoint path="/list"             length="133" body='[{"id":1,"timestamp":"2023-08-26T01:00:00Z"},{"id":2,"timestamp":"2023-08-27T01:00:00Z"},{"id":3,"timestamp":"2023-08-28T02:14:26Z"}]'/>
    <endpoint path="/trigger-backup"   length="18"  body='{"triggered":true}'/>
    <endpoint path="/download/2"       length="26"  body='{"id":2,"data":"SGVsbG8="}'/>

    <!--
        The checker will verify:
          * The mock server is (or was) listening on 0.0.0.0:9090.
          * The log file exists at /home/user/backup_test/backup_api_test.log.
          * The log file has 5 lines.  Each line must match:
              ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z\|(GET|POST) [^|]+\|200\|[0-9]+$
            and the <BYTES> part must match the “length” values above for the
            corresponding endpoint.
          * The summary.txt file exists with the five exact lines, no extra
            whitespace or trailing blank lines.
    -->

    <expected-log-path>/home/user/backup_test/backup_api_test.log</expected-log-path>
    <expected-summary-path>/home/user/backup_test/summary.txt</expected-summary-path>
name: task_000000_ea5c9ead
